Face anonymization approaches the problem by erasing and replacing the face region.
Early methods used a database of real faces to create new identities.
Recent methods utilize generative models to synthesize fake identities.
DeepPrivacy is a prominent example in the field of reconstructing anonymized faces.
DeepPrivacy is a pioneering work in the field of anonymizing faces.
It reconstructs the missing face using masked face and facial landmarks.
The distribution suffers from bias due to being conditioned solely on training data.
CIAGAN is another method for generating faces using masks and landmarks.
CIAGAN tends to generate faces with duplicated identities due to one-hot vector limitations.
RePaint is a recent approach based on diffusion models.

RePaint struggles to maintain the utility of faces and is sensitive to input distributions.

Password allows for anonymized faces to be de-anonymized with a password.

GDPR (Voigt and Von dem Bussche 2017) protects pseudonymous data and opposes reversible anonymization.
